result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Date picker open on Google Flights round-trip search. April 22, 2026 is selected as departure; May 1, 2026 is selected as return. “Done” button visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
Round trip; Reset; Wed, Apr 22; Fri, May 1; April 2026; May 2026; Done; 1 $730
selected_candidate:
The “Search” button/label was the intended click target, but the visible state shows the date picker overlay instead of search results.
constraint_evidence:
- Round-trip flight search: supported (visible “Round trip” and URL context)
- Depart from Los Angeles: not_visible (not shown in screenshot; only URL context implies route)
- Arrive in Zurich: not_visible (not shown in screenshot; only URL context implies route)
- Depart on April 22, 2026: supported (visible “Wed, Apr 22” and highlighted 22 in April 2026)
- Return on May 1, 2026: supported (visible “Fri, May 1” and highlighted 1 in May 2026)
- Search action clicked: supported (action context says click Search)
- Flight results loaded: not_visible (still on date picker, no results list)
action_target:
Click the Search span/button to submit the selected round-trip dates.
success_signals:
Search results page appears with flights for Los Angeles ↔ Zurich and the chosen dates.
failure_signals:
Date picker remains open; no results page; dates or route not applied.
uncertainty:
The route fields are not visible in this screenshot, so origin/destination cannot be directly verified here.

args.task: navigate: Search for a round-trip flight from Los Angeles to Zurich, departing on April 22, 2026, and returning on May 1, 2026.

args.query: Google Flights round-trip date picker search button clicked but results page not loaded; date picker still open after selecting departure and return dates

evidence.claim: The selected step clicked the Search control while the Google Flights date picker overlay was still open.
High-detail VLM reads the visible state as the date picker open with Wed, Apr 22 and Fri, May 1 selected, and says the visible state still does not show a results list.
The same high-detail VLM notes that flight results are not visible at the clicked step; it is still the picker overlay.
The trajectory's final screenshot summary shows Google Flights results for Los Angeles to Zürich with prices from $730, indicating the search did complete after the click.
No comparable validated success run was returned for this task, and no validated eval-case precedent was found for this exact pattern.

suggested_followups.label: Check route fields
suggested_followups.message: If you want, I can inspect the route entry fields at the final results state to confirm Los Angeles and Zürich were set correctly.

suggested_followups.label: Inspect a neighboring step
suggested_followups.message: I can also inspect step 18 at high detail to verify the final results page and whether the click on Search transitioned properly.
